figure.title: Sparse Write Request Straddling a Beat Boundary
figure.purpose: Shows an example of a sparse write request with scattered byte enables crossing from one beat to the next.

technical_summary: ReqAddr starts at DW14 in BEAT 0.
The request straddles the boundary into BEAT 1.
Byte enables are specified for individual bytes across DW14, DW15 in BEAT 0, and DW0 in BEAT 1.
Only bytes with an active '1' enable are written.

packet_layout.description: First beat containing the start of the sparse write
Second beat containing the end of the sparse write

critical_facts: Sparse writes can straddle beat boundaries just like contiguous reads/writes, maintaining byte-level enable granularity.
